title: Systems Engineer, Workplace M365
description:
Job Overview

We are seeking an experienced Workplace Engineer to support and enhance the modern workplace environment. This role focuses on deploying, supporting, and optimizing end-user technologies to ensure a productive, secure, and reliable user experience.

The successful candidate will provide advanced technical support across Endpoint, Cloud, and collaboration platforms, and play a key role in supporting modern workplace initiatives. This role requires strong troubleshooting skills, hands-on technical capability, and the ability to work closely with users and cross-functional IT teams.

Key Responsibilities

  • Provide timely and effective support by responding to service desk tickets and technical incidents
  • Monitor and respond to alerts from endpoint and device management platforms
  • Deliver second and third-level support for end-user issues across applications, hardware, operating systems, and connectivity
  • Install, configure, and maintain workplace hardware, software, and productivity tools
  • Prepare and deploy workstations for new joiners, including system setup, software installation, and peripheral configuration
  • Perform regular system maintenance, patching, and updates to ensure performance, security, and stability
  • Work closely with other IT teams to troubleshoot and resolve complex technical issues
  • Communicate clearly with end users to understand requirements and provide appropriate solutions
  • Support and contribute to ongoing improvements in workplace tools, processes, and automation

Requirements

Qualifications

  • Diploma or Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related discipline
  • Minimum 5 years of experience in end-user computing, workplace engineering, or a similar role
  • Strong working knowledge of Windows and macOS operating systems
  • Experience supporting cloud productivity platforms such as Microsoft 365 and Office 365
  • Familiarity with cloud services including Azure and AWS
  • Hands-on experience with endpoint management solutions such as Intune, SCCM, Ivanti, or equivalent tools
  • Experience supporting virtual desktop solutions such as VMware Horizon, Windows 365 Cloud PC, Workspace ONE, or Citrix
  • Good understanding of networking fundamentals including TCP/IP, DNS, and DHCP
  • Experience with mobile device management (MDM) solutions
  • Knowledge of IT service management frameworks such as ITIL

Advanced / Value-Add Skills

  • Experience with collaboration and security platforms such as SharePoint, Microsoft Defender, Microsoft Purview, DLP, and AWS Connect
  • Exposure to automation tools such as Power Automate
  • Scripting experience, particularly PowerShell

Certifications

  • Relevant certifications such as CompTIA A+, Microsoft 365 Fundamentals, or Microsoft Endpoint Administrator Associate
  • Additional certifications such as Azure Administrator Associate, SharePoint, AWS Cloud Practitioner, or AI-related certifications are an advantage

Personal Attributes

  • Strong analytical and troubleshooting skills
  • Good communication and interpersonal abilities
  • Able to work independently and collaboratively in a team environment
  • Willingness to learn and adapt to new and evolving technologies

Please send your detailed resume in MS Word format to resume@goldtecHRs.com with

  • Education Level
  • Working experiences
  • Each employment background
  • Reason for leaving each employment
  • Last drawn salary
  • Expected salary
  • Date of availability
